title: Decadent Yeasty Brazilian Pears Tart
description: A rich, yeasty Brazilian tart featuring poached pears, crispy lardons, and a hint of cinnamon. Perfect for a romantic dinner date.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, granulated sugar, and yeast.
In a separate bowl, whisk together warm milk, eggs, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until elastic.
Transfer the dough to a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch (23 cm) tart pan with a removable bottom.
Peel, core, and halve the pear. Poach the pear in a saucepan of simmering water with the brown sugar and vanilla extract, for 10 minutes, or until tender.
Arrange the poached pear halves on the tart dough, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Cut the lardons into small pieces and cook in a skillet over medium heat, until crispy.
Arrange the crispy lardons on top of the pear halves.
Bake the tart for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before serving.
